rationale: The query's/hyp's core engine is a generating-function-based self-consistent branching (cascade) calculation from an arbitrary degree/functionality distribution to derive a phase transition (giant component / gel point) and the full size distribution from generating-function derivatives; the candidate instead performs an empirical statistical description of an actually-reconstructed wiring diagram (degree distributions, synaptic multiplicities, small-world/motif statistics) plus linear-systems modeling of signal propagation — no generating function, self-consistent composition, or percolation-threshold derivation appears, so the machinery does not cohere. On the object side, the candidate is a real-world network (C. elegans neuronal wiring) analyzed with the same graph-theoretic descriptive toolkit (degree distributions, small-world properties) that the query paper itself applies to its own worked examples (WWW, collaboration graphs) — this is the query's own field (empirical/theoretical complex-network science) studying yet another real-world graph, not a transfer into the sought target field of polymer chemistry/gelation, so no genuine cross-field transfer has occurred."
